title: Problem 1 (Dvořák, Norin and Postle)
statement_text: Does there exist $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every planar graph $G$ and assignment $L$ of lists of size (a) five in general, (b) four if $G$ is triangle-free, (c) three if $G$ has girth at least five is (weighted) $\varepsilon$-flexible?
context_text: Dvořák, Norin and Postle introduced the notion of $\varepsilon$-flexibility for graph classes and proved flexibility for planar graphs with lists of size 6 and for planar graphs of girth at least 5 with lists of size 4. They raised Problem 1 as a natural next step, asking whether the list sizes matching the choosability threshold suffice for flexibility. The present paper resolves part (b) affirmatively (Theorem 2); parts (a) and (c) remain open.
paper_contribution: Proves part (b) in the affirmative: there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every triangle-free planar graph with an assignment of lists of size four is weighted $\varepsilon$-flexible.
notes: Parts (a) and (c) of Problem 1 are not resolved by this paper and remain open. The tail of the extracted text is garbled PDF noise (a list of arXiv IDs); no further mathematical content could be recovered from it.
